card.intuition: A high-performance, in-memory data store shared across multiple servers to improve data retrieval speed and reduce load on primary databases.
card.analogy: A shared bookshelf in an office: instead of every employee driving home (Database) to check a fact, important books are kept in a common room (Cache). If one person updates a book, everyone sees the new version immediately.
when_to_use: Storing session data for web users
Reducing Latency for frequent database queries
Implementing Rate Limiters or Pub/Sub systems
gotchas: Cache Invalidation: keeping the cache perfectly in sync with the database is one of the hardest problems in CS ('There are only two hard things in Computer Science: cache invalidation and naming things').
Hot Keys: if one specific piece of data is requested millions of times per second (like a celebrity's profile), it can overwhelm the specific cache server holding it.

exercise.prompt: Identify the caching strategy where data is written to the cache FIRST, and the database is updated asynchronously later.
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "back"
assert check("Back")
print("PASS")
